title: Canonical Rabies PEP Decision Flow
description: Skeleton decision flow for rabies post-exposure prophylaxis. Merges WHO SEARO and Ontario (ON) clinical logic into a single canonical structure. All branch conditions are placeholders for manual completion.

sources.title: WHO SEARO Rabies PEP Decision Tree
sources.citation: WHO South-East Asia. Decide with Confidence: Rabies Post-Exposure Prophylaxis Decision Tree.

design_principles: Each node represents one clinical decision point or assessment gate.
Nodes consume intake answers and derived facts; they do not re-ask questions.
Branch conditions reference derived_fact_ids, not raw intake question IDs.
Policy overlays modify branch behaviour without altering the core flow structure.
Endpoints are terminal nodes that produce a recommendation and action set.
All placeholder logic is marked TODO_manual_* for human authoring.

intake_dependency.description: This flow consumes answers from the canonical rabies intake checklist. Intake question IDs are referenced in node input_fields as c01–c49.

nodes.label: Start / Triage
nodes.description: Entry point. Checks whether this is a new assessment or a continuation of existing RPEP.

nodes.label: Animal Species Gate
nodes.description: Determines whether the animal is a mammal, and if so, whether it is a bat. Non-mammals exit immediately.
nodes.manual_notes: c05 (is rodent) and c07 (mammal type enum) both contribute to host taxon derivation. See derived fact df_host_taxon.

nodes.label: Bat Exposure Assessment
nodes.description: Evaluates bat-specific exposure details per ON bat sub-tree. Determines whether bat contact can be ruled out, whether a bat was actually seen, and whether the bat is available/testable/desiccated.

branches.condition: TODO_manual_condition: desiccated dead bat, no saliva/tissue exposure
branches.label: Desiccated bat → no PEP

branches.condition: TODO_manual_condition: bat not available, minimal/uncertain contact only
branches.label: Minimal contact → consult

branches.condition: TODO_manual_condition: bat not available, contact not ruled out
branches.label: Bat exposure confirmed → assess patient

nodes.manual_notes: This sub-tree is primarily ON-derived. WHO handles bats via wound category III auto-escalation and bat_policy_supersedes. The two approaches must be reconciled here.

nodes.label: Relevant Exposure Determination
nodes.description: Determines whether a clinically relevant exposure occurred. Evaluates both ON exposure type (bite, scratch, saliva, neural tissue) and WHO wound category (I/II/III).

branches.condition: TODO_manual_condition: no relevant exposure (category I, no skin break, no mucous membrane)
branches.label: No relevant exposure → no PEP

nodes.manual_notes: Must preserve WHO category II vs III distinction — this determines RIG eligibility downstream. c15 and c16 may produce conflicting signals; reconciliation logic needed.

nodes.label: Exposure Timing Assessment
nodes.description: Evaluates time since exposure. WHO 10-day rule: if >10 days and animal is alive and healthy, PEP may be discontinued or not started.

branches.condition: TODO_manual_condition: >10 days and animal alive and healthy
branches.label: 10-day rule met → no PEP

branches.condition: TODO_manual_condition: within window or animal status unknown
branches.label: Continue assessment

nodes.manual_notes: The 10-day rule applies primarily to dogs, cats, and ferrets per WHO footnote 1. Other species require 14-day observation. This node must be taxon-aware.

nodes.label: High-Priority Victim and Exposure Features
nodes.description: WHO high-priority group: any-yes from child <14, multiple/deep wounds, highly innervated area triggers immediate PEP start regardless of downstream assessment.

branches.label: High priority → fast-track to patient assessment

nodes.manual_notes: High-priority flag should persist through the rest of the flow and influence endpoint urgency even if a downstream node routes to observe/test.

nodes.label: Animal Clinical Signs Assessment
nodes.description: Evaluates concerning animal features: rabies-compatible signs/behaviour, death or disappearance within 10 days, multiple victims. Also evaluates ownership tier (owned, stray, feral/wild) and provocation.

branches.condition: TODO_manual_condition: no concerning features, animal vaccinated, provoked exposure
branches.label: Lower risk → check availability

nodes.manual_notes: WHO concerning features group uses any-yes logic. ON behaviour assessment has distinct footnote criteria (iv). Animal vaccination status (c32) modifies risk but does not eliminate it.

nodes.label: Animal Availability and Observation Pathway
nodes.description: Determines whether the animal is available for observation or testing. Routes to testing if available, or to patient assessment if not.
nodes.manual_notes: This is the merged animal availability gate. WHO 'available for quarantine' and ON 'identified and located' have subtly different meanings. Policy overlay po01 modifies feasibility.

nodes.label: Animal Testing and Observation
nodes.description: Evaluates whether the animal has been or can be tested for rabies, and what the test result is. Also handles the observation-period pathway.

branches.condition: TODO_manual_condition: not tested, observation not feasible
branches.label: Cannot test/observe → assess patient

nodes.manual_notes: WHO footnote 4: risk assessment is point-in-time. If animal status changes, reassessment is needed. Test-negative exit requires WOAH-recommended method per WHO.

nodes.label: Prior Rabies Vaccination Assessment
nodes.description: Evaluates patient's prior rabies vaccination or PEP history. Determines whether a shortened regimen (no RIG, fewer doses) is appropriate.

branches.condition: TODO_manual_condition: previously vaccinated with valid series
branches.label: Previously vaccinated → modified regimen

branches.condition: TODO_manual_condition: no prior vaccination or unknown
branches.label: Vaccine-naive → full regimen

nodes.manual_notes: ON has a complex sub-tree for prior vaccination with dose counting, interval validation, and serology requirements. WHO uses simpler yes/no prior vaccination + PEP <3 months. Both must be represented. ON footnote v: validity of vaccine received outside Canada may be uncertain.

nodes.label: Current Patient Modifiers
nodes.description: Evaluates patient-specific modifiers: immunocompromise (current and historical), WHO high-risk composite flag, pregnancy, allergies.

branches.condition: TODO_manual_condition: immunocompromised — may invalidate prior vaccination, requires serology
branches.label: Immunocompromised → route with modifier

nodes.manual_notes: ON distinguishes c43 (any immunocompromise) from c44 (moderate-to-severe). c45 (immunocompromised at time of prior vaccination) affects validity of prior series — must feed back to df_prior_vaccination_reliable. WHO footnote 9 defines high-risk composite.

nodes.label: Wound Category and Treatment Intensity Routing
nodes.description: Routes to appropriate endpoint based on WHO wound category (determines RIG eligibility), host risk tier, accumulated flags, and patient modifiers.

branches.condition: TODO_manual_condition: WHO category III or high-priority or high-risk host, no prior vaccination
branches.label: Full PEP + RIG

branches.condition: TODO_manual_condition: WHO category III, previously vaccinated with valid series
branches.label: Shortened PEP, no RIG

branches.condition: TODO_manual_condition: WHO category II, no prior vaccination
branches.label: PEP without RIG

nodes.manual_notes: This is the central treatment-intensity routing node. WHO category II vs III distinction is safety-critical: Category III = vaccine + RIG; Category II = vaccine only. Must also account for prior vaccination status modifying the regimen.

nodes.label: Resource Availability Check
nodes.description: Checks whether RIG/RmAbs and vaccine are available. Routes to appropriate endpoint variant based on resource constraints.

branches.condition: TODO_manual_condition: RIG limited, patient is high-risk per WHO footnote 9
branches.label: Prioritize RIG + contact PH

branches.condition: TODO_manual_condition: RIG limited, patient is not high-risk
branches.label: Vaccine without RIG + contact PH

nodes.manual_notes: WHO resource-limited pathway. WHO footnote 9 defines high-risk patients who should receive priority RIG allocation. ON operates in a resource-available context but still has biologics coordination (footnote x).

nodes.label: Continue Existing RPEP
nodes.description: Patient has already started RPEP. Determine if the series should continue, be modified, or if a new assessment is needed for a re-exposure.

branches.condition: TODO_manual_condition: same exposure, series in progress
branches.label: Continue current series

branches.condition: TODO_manual_condition: new exposure during existing series
branches.label: New exposure → full reassessment

policy_overlays.label: Animal Observation Feasibility
policy_overlays.description: Whether 10-day (dog/cat/ferret) or 14-day (other species) animal observation is operationally feasible in the jurisdiction.

policy_overlays.label: Animal Testing Availability
policy_overlays.description: Whether WOAH-recommended rabies testing is available and reliable in the jurisdiction.

policy_overlays.label: Veterinary Assessment Feasibility
policy_overlays.description: Whether veterinary clinical assessment of the animal (for rabies-compatible signs) is available.

policy_overlays.label: Public Health Consultation Availability
policy_overlays.description: Whether public health consultation is available and required prior to RPEP initiation or for travel-related exposures.

policy_overlays.label: Biologics Availability
policy_overlays.description: Whether rabies vaccine and RIG/RmAbs are available at the treating facility or can be arranged.

derived_facts.label: Animal is a mammal
Animal is a bat (confirmed or suspected)
Resolved host taxon ID for catalog lookup
derived_facts.derivation: TODO_manual_logic: c07 enum → taxon; c05 fallback → small_rodent; c06 fallback → bat

derived_facts.label: Bat contact can be ruled out
Bat is available for rabies testing
Bat is dead and desiccated (no residual risk)
Confidence level that bat exposure occurred
Clinically relevant exposure occurred
derived_facts.derivation: TODO_manual_logic: c15 any item selected → true; OR c16 category II/III → true

derived_facts.label: WHO wound/exposure category (I, II, or III)
derived_facts.derivation: TODO_manual_logic: pass-through from c16; if absent, derive from c15 items

derived_facts.label: Detailed exposure types (preserved from ON multiselect)
Exposure occurred more than 10 days ago
derived_facts.derivation: TODO_manual_logic: c18 direct; or compute from c02 vs current date

derived_facts.label: Animal confirmed alive at 10-day mark
Victim is younger than 14 years
Victim has multiple or deep wounds
Wound is in a highly innervated area (head, neck, face, genitals, hands)
derived_facts.derivation: TODO_manual_logic: c22 direct; or derive from c17 multiselect containing head/face/neck/genitals/hands

derived_facts.label: WHO high-priority any-yes composite flag
derived_facts.derivation: TODO_manual_logic: any of df_is_child, df_has_severe_wounds, df_highly_innervated_site → true

derived_facts.label: Animal showed signs/behaviour compatible with rabies
Animal died or disappeared within 10 days
Animal bit 2 or more people or animals
Animal ownership classification
derived_facts.derivation: TODO_manual_logic: c27=yes → feral_or_wild; c28=yes → stray; neither → owned

derived_facts.label: Animal up-to-date on rabies vaccination
Animal was provoked to bite or scratch
WHO concerning animal features any-yes composite flag
derived_facts.derivation: TODO_manual_logic: any of df_animal_rabies_signs=yes, df_animal_died_disappeared=yes, df_animal_multiple_victims=yes → true; any=unknown → indeterminate

derived_facts.label: Animal available for observation or testing
Animal has been tested for rabies
Animal rabies test result
Patient has prior rabies vaccination history
Patient completed PEP/RPEP within past 3 months
Patient has a valid prior vaccination series (per ON interval criteria + serology)
Number of valid prior vaccine doses (ON interval-validated)
Prior vaccination is considered immunologically reliable (accounts for immunocompromise at time of prior vaccination)
derived_facts.derivation: TODO_manual_logic: documentation available AND (adequate serology OR not immunocompromised at time of prior vaccination)

derived_facts.label: Patient is currently immunocompromised (any degree)
Patient is currently moderate-to-severely immunocompromised or on chloroquine/hydroxychloroquine
Patient was immunocompromised at time of prior vaccination
WHO high-risk composite flag (footnote 9)
derived_facts.derivation: TODO_manual_logic: WHO footnote 9 criteria: multiple bites, deep wounds, highly innervated sites, severe immunodeficiency, confirmed/probable rabid animal, bat exposure

derived_facts.label: Patient is currently pregnant
Patient has known allergy to rabies vaccine or RIG components
RIG/RmAbs availability is limited
Host species risk tier from catalog lookup
derived_facts.derivation: TODO_manual_logic: resolved from df_host_taxon via zone-specific host profile catalog

decision_tables.label: WHO Wound Category → Treatment Intensity
decision_tables.description: Maps WHO wound category + prior vaccination status → vaccine regimen + RIG eligibility.

decision_tables.label: Host Taxon + Zone → Risk Tier + Trigger Weight
decision_tables.description: Resolves host species against epi zone catalog to determine risk tier and PEP trigger weight.

decision_tables.label: Prior Vaccination Status → Regimen Modification
decision_tables.description: Determines how prior vaccination history modifies the PEP regimen (shortened schedule, RIG omission, serology requirements).

endpoints.label: No Rabies PEP Indicated
endpoints.description: Assessment complete: rabies PEP is not indicated for this exposure.
action_set: Document assessment in patient chart
Provide wound care as appropriate
Advise patient on signs/symptoms warranting reassessment
endpoints.exit_conditions: TODO_manual_conditions: list of node paths that lead here

endpoints.label: Observe / Test Animal and Reassess
endpoints.description: Animal is available and should be observed (10/14 days) or tested. PEP may be deferred pending results, or started and discontinued if animal tests negative / remains healthy.
action_set: Arrange animal observation or testing via public health / animal control
Thorough wound washing with soap and water (15 min) and antiseptic
Consider initiating PEP if observation/testing will be delayed
Schedule reassessment based on observation/test timeline
Notify public health authority
endpoints.manual_notes: If PEP is started during observation and animal is confirmed healthy/negative, PEP can be discontinued.

endpoints.label: Start / Arrange RPEP
endpoints.description: Rabies PEP is indicated. Regimen (vaccine doses, RIG eligibility) depends on wound category, prior vaccination status, and immunocompromise status.
action_set: Thorough wound washing with soap and water (15 min) and antiseptic
Administer rabies vaccine per regimen
Administer RIG if indicated (WHO category III, vaccine-naive, infiltrate wound site)
Schedule follow-up doses
Notify public health authority

regimen_variants.immunocompromised: TODO_manual_detail: full PEP + RIG + serology at 2-4 weeks

endpoints.label: Start / Arrange RPEP — Contact Public Health
endpoints.description: Rabies PEP is indicated and public health coordination is required prior to or concurrent with initiation (e.g., biologics delivery, travel-related exposure, resource-limited context).
action_set: Contact public health authority by phone before or concurrent with PEP initiation
Thorough wound washing with soap and water (15 min) and antiseptic
Administer rabies vaccine per regimen when available
Administer RIG if indicated and available
Have patient weight available for RIG dosing
Provide call-back number for biologics coordination
endpoints.manual_notes: ON footnote x: Ontario will arrange delivery of biologics, typically within 2–4 hours.

endpoints.label: Continue Existing RPEP Series
endpoints.description: Patient has already started RPEP for this exposure. Continue the current series as scheduled.
action_set: Confirm current dose schedule
Administer next scheduled dose
Assess for adverse reactions
Coordinate follow-up with public health
endpoints.manual_notes: ON footnote xi: Ontario will coordinate follow-up doses.

endpoints.label: Expert / Public Health Consultation Required
endpoints.description: Clinical scenario requires expert or public health consultation before a definitive recommendation can be made. May apply to: conflicting data, unusual species, travel-related exposure, uncertain vaccination history, immunocompromise interactions.
action_set: Thorough wound washing with soap and water (15 min) and antiseptic
Contact public health or infectious disease specialist
Consider initiating PEP if consultation will be delayed and risk is non-trivial
Document reason for consultation referral

validation_rules.description: Every intake question (c01–c49) must be referenced by at least one derived fact or one node input_fields list.
Every derived fact must be consumed by at least one node decision_logic or branch condition.
Every node must have at least one branch that leads to an endpoint (no dead ends).
Every endpoint must be reachable from the entry node via at least one path.
WHO wound category II vs III distinction must be preserved through to endpoint regimen selection (not collapsed to boolean).
ON bat sub-tree (desiccated bat exit, minimal contact consult path) must be represented as distinct branches.
Prior vaccination history must modify regimen at endpoints (shortened schedule, RIG omission) — not be discarded.
Immunocompromise at time of prior vaccination (c45/df_immunocompromised_at_prior_vax) must feed back into df_prior_vaccination_reliable.
Geography must resolve to a known epi zone or flag as unknown — no silent defaults.
All source traces must account for every WHO question (q01–q22) and every ON question (q01–q27) at least once across all nodes.

notes: This is a skeleton file. All decision_logic and branch conditions are placeholders (TODO_manual_*) for manual completion.
Node ordering reflects a sensible clinical assessment sequence but is not strictly linear — branches may skip nodes or loop back.
The derived_facts layer exists to decouple raw intake answers from decision logic. Nodes should reference derived facts, not raw question IDs, in their branch conditions.
Policy overlays are jurisdiction-specific modifiers applied on top of the core flow. They should not alter the node structure, only the branch conditions and thresholds.
Decision tables are provided for the most complex multi-variable routing decisions. They are intended to be filled in as lookup matrices.

The audit from the intake wiring review identified specific semantic-loss risks (WHO category collapse, geography silent defaults, desiccated bat, positive test). This flow skeleton addresses each of those as distinct branching paths.
The current engine (core/engine.ts) implements a subset of this flow. The skeleton is designed to eventually replace or extend the engine rules.
